task_completion.description: Create a sketch with a 32mm circular head centered at the origin and a 12mm wide handle extending at least 100mm along the X-axis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: FreeCAD sketcher view showing a circle centered at the origin (diameter 32mm or radius 16mm) and parallel lines forming a handle extending to the right along the horizontal axis.

task_completion.description: Incorporate a 15.0mm wide jaw cutout angled at 15 degrees relative to the handle.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Sketcher view showing an open cutout in the circular head. Look for dimension constraints showing a 15mm gap width and a 15-degree angle relative to the horizontal handle.

task_completion.description: Extrude the final profile to a thickness of 2.0mm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: FreeCAD 'Pad' or 'Extrude' dialog box visible with a length/thickness parameter set to 2.0mm, or the 3D view showing the sketch converted into a thin solid.

task_completion.description: Complete the design as a single solid 3D model of a cone wrench.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeCAD 3D viewport displays a completed, single solid object resembling a thin wrench with an angled open jaw and a long handle.
FreeCAD save dialog showing the path '/home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/' and filename 'cone_wrench.FCStd', or the main window title bar displaying 'cone_wrench.FCStd'.

integrity.description: The agent used the FreeCAD graphical interface to design the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with FreeCAD's toolbars, sketcher, and 3D viewport, rather than just terminal commands or text editors.

integrity.description: The model was built from scratch using standard CAD operations.
integrity.visual_evidence: The FreeCAD Combo View (Tree View) shows a logical progression of features, such as 'Sketch' followed by 'Pad', indicating the model was actively constructed.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply download or open a pre-existing file.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is visual evidence of the sketching and extrusion process occurring step-by-step in the screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected filename: cone_wrench.FCStd. Target thickness: 2.0mm. Target jaw gap: 15.0mm. A 15mm bicycle cone wrench is a real tool with a jaw opening width of 15.0mm and standard thickness of 2.0mm.
